learning_outcomes.description: Understand contemporary practice in site planning, structural and environmental building system design and construction detailing
Resolve an integrated design proposal that demonstrates code compliance and technical competence.
Individually and collaboratively evaluate alternative technical solutions for a sustainable architectural design
Individually and collaboratively prepare a BIM model and a set of technical drawings.

assessments.assessment_title: Assignment 1: Feasibility + Concept Design Report (group submission)
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale: This group assignment will include the advanced resolution of the base of a commercial building and preliminary structural grid application to the tower, presented as a Feasibility + Concept Design Report.

Students will be required to:

·     Undertake site analysis;

·     Respond to Town Planning requirements;

·     Design in accordance with NCC Part D and carparking standards;

·     Test options for lift and stair core locations and configurations;

·     Investigate structural systems and apply appropriate structural grids. 

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support, prior to submission.

Peer Evaluation: This assessment task involves groupwork and will include a peer evaluation submission.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale: This individual assignment is a work in progress submission of tasks completed during class in weeks 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9. Each week will focus on a different aspect of the project and will set out a specific task to be worked on by all students. Topics will include:

·     Mechanical, Electrical, Hydraulic and Fire services;

·     Façade sun-shading design principles;

·     Glazing configurations and technologies; 

·     Sustainability and NCC Part J;

·     Low Carbon Circular Economy.  

At the close of each class, students are to submit their work in progress in the form of drawings, sketches, and other material as evidence of the progress of their project's development and their authorship of the work.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale: 

This individual assignment will include the further development of the work started in class in weeks 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9, and integrated into a unified project. The focus of Assignment 3 will be to lock in key design moves for the core, podium, tower, façade and sun shading. Key drawings and details will be developed to a level of presentation suitable for communicating design intent.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale: 

This individual assignment will include refining the project documentation based on the design completed over the course of the semester, including feedback from previous Assignments and discussions with tutors. A final coordinated set of developed technical working drawings are to be submitted, suitable for the purposes of tendering the project to a builder.

National Standard of Competency for Architects (NSCA) (2021) Performance Criteria: 

This assessment task addresses the NSCA (2021) Performance Criteria 12, 33, 35, 39, 44, 45, 46 and 47. More information on the NSCA is available here: https://aaca.org.au/national-standard-of-competency-for-architects/2021nsca/
